subject: Computer Networking
title: What Is a Protocol?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on What Is a Protocol?.

hook.text: Two computers connect. How do they agree on language, speed, and format? They follow a protocol.

buildup.text: A protocol is a set of rules. It defines how data is packaged, sent, received, and understood.

discovery.text: HTTP for web pages, SMTP for email, FTP for files — each service has its own protocol.

twist.text: Protocols stack on top of each other. HTTP uses TCP, which uses IP. Layers make complexity manageable.

climax.text: Without agreed protocols, every device would speak a different language. The internet would be chaos.

punchline.text: Protocols are the grammar of the internet.

quiz.question: What is a network protocol?
options: A set of rules for how data is communicated
